text: Open access research data are frequently used to define metrics for validation studies, to serve as educational examples and to facilitate the reproduction of experiments. Therefore, there is a strong need for open access research data represented in the form of high-quality algorithms using modern technology. Insight ToolKit version 4 (ITK4) provides such modern representation of widely used open access image registration techniques. ITK4 has been significantly enhanced with respect to the ITK4 ancestors. Multi-dimensional images and multi-variate objective functions are supported. High-dimensional deformation fields representing complex local transformations can be easily handled together with low-dimensional affine transformations. A large number of reusable metrics and reusable composite transforms allow to connect any type of geometric transform in a sequence. Furthermore, many metrics and all common optimizers are multi-threaded. Metrics and optimizers have heuristically-derived smart scaling formulas for vastly different parameter types such as rotations and translations, and step-size control for gradient based optimizers. As a result, the frequently needed, yet tedious and often manual, step of selecting optimal values for the parameters of the registration methods is greatly diminished, and researchers can concentrate on developing new registration algorithms and comparing them. ITK4 provides a new, robust and highly validated reference platform for reproducible research on new registration algorithms and includes all the components required for performing validation studies. We used the reference validation study on pediatric brain segmentations as an application example.
